codingTopics: Audio and content delivery: streaming, offline caching, prefetch
Mobile data structures: LRU caches, playlists, queues
Strings and search: content-catalog search, prefix and autocomplete
Personalization: ranking and recommendation over user history
Streaming: session and progress event processing
systemDesignTopics: Design audio content delivery with offline downloads and resumable playback
Design enterprise B2B integration for employer-sponsored access with SSO
Design member-journey personalization and content recommendation
Design a progress-and-streak tracking system across devices

coding: Implement an LRU cache for offline-downloaded meditation audio with a storage cap. Discuss eviction and complexity.
Given a member's play history, recommend the next session by ranking a content catalog. Discuss the scoring approach.
Build autocomplete over the content library (titles, teachers, topics) using a prefix trie.
Given a stream of listening-progress events, compute per-member daily streaks with correct time-zone handling.
behavioral: Headspace's mission is mental health. Tell me about a time mission or user wellbeing shaped a technical choice.
Describe a time you designed for accessibility or a vulnerable user.
Give an example of collaborating with content or clinical teams to ship.
Tell me about a time you owned a mobile reliability or performance problem.
systemDesign: Design audio content delivery with offline downloads and resumable playback for mobile.
Design enterprise B2B integration for employer-sponsored access, including SSO.
Design member-journey personalization that recommends the right content at the right moment.
values: Headspace leads with compassion. Describe a time you prioritized a user's wellbeing over an engagement metric.
How do you balance business (B2B) needs with a member-first product experience?
Tell me about a time you handled sensitive user data such as health or wellness data responsibly.
other: How does Headspace compare to Calm?

Headspace Interview Guide (2026): Mental Health and Meditation

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Headspace
headspace.com ↗

Headspace is one of the leading mental health and meditation platforms — merged with Ginger to form Headspace Health in 2021. The interview emphasizes audio content delivery, B2B/B2C platform integration, and the unique mission of mental wellness at scale.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design audio content delivery with offline downloads

- Design enterprise (B2B) integration for employer-sponsored access

- Design member journey personalization

- Coding: medium DSA, often with mobile or content framing

- Behavioral: mission-driven (mental wellness), customer focus

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $150K–$185K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$285K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Swift / Kotlin (mobile) and Node.js (backend)

- Understand audio streaming and offline caching

- Brush up on enterprise SSO and member management

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Headspace remote-friendly?

Distributed across Santa Monica (HQ), San Francisco, London. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Headspace compare to Calm?

Calm has slightly larger consumer base. Headspace has stronger B2B/clinical positioning. Comp is comparable.

What is the engineering culture?

Mission-driven, calm pace, strong work-life balance. Mental health awareness pervades the culture.
